code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about universities (or related entities)
    into a single fluent, complex sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject to enable subject chaining
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase template
    templates = {
        "city": "is located in {object}",
        "country": "is in {object}",
        "latinName": "has the Latin name {object}",
        "nickname": "is nicknamed {object}",
        "rector": "has rector {object}",
        "academicStaffSize": "has {object} academic staff",
        "established": "was established in {object}",
        "state": "is in the state of {object}",
        "governmentType": "has government type {object}",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has {object} to its northeast",
        "dean": "has dean {object}",
        "location": "is located in {object}",
        "numberOfStudents": "has {object} students",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with {object}",
        "director": "has director {object}",
        "motto": "has motto \"{object}\"",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has {object} postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has {object} undergraduate students",
        "officialSchoolColour": "has official school colour {object}",
        "outlookRanking": "is ranked {object} in the Outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "offers {object}",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given technical campus status by {object}",
        "founder": "was founded by {object}",
        "leader": "is led by {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {object}",
        "religion": "has religion {object}",
        "headquarter": "has headquarter in {object}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {object}",
        "river": "has river {object}",
        "hasToItsWest": "has {object} to its west",
        "anthem": "has anthem \"{object}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": "has ethnic group {object}",
        "legislature": "has legislature {object}",
        "sportGoverningBody": "has sport governing body {object}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {object}",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors {object}",
        "capital": "has capital {object}",
        "patronSaint": "has patron saint {object}",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has {object} to its northwest",
        "campus": "has campus at {object}",
        "president": "has president {object}",
        "staff": "has {object} staff members",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has {object} doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is at an elevation of {object} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {object}",
        "longName": "has long name {object}",
    }

    # Find the primary subject (most triples or university-related)
    primary_subject = max(subject_triples.keys(), key=lambda s: len(subject_triples[s]))
    primary_triples = subject_triples[primary_subject]

    # Build main clause and relative clauses
    main_clauses = []
    relative_clauses = []

    for t in primary_triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "has {predicate} {object}")
        clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
        # Prioritize location predicates for main clause
        if t.predicate in ["city", "country", "location", "state"]:
            main_clauses.append(clause)
        else:
            relative_clauses.append(clause)

    # Build the sentence with proper structure
    if main_clauses:
        sentence = f"{primary_subject} {' and '.join(main_clauses)}"
        if relative_clauses:
            # Join relative clauses with appropriate connectors
            if len(relative_clauses) == 1:
                sentence += f", {relative_clauses[0]}."
            else:
                sentence += ", which " + ", which ".join(relative_clauses) + "."
    else:
        # Fallback to all clauses
        all_clauses = []
        for t in primary_triples:
            tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "has {predicate} {object}")
            clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
            all_clauses.append(clause)
        sentence = f"{primary_subject} {' and '.join(all_clauses)}."

    # Handle other subjects with relative clauses
    other_subjects = [s for s in subject_triples.keys() if s != primary_subject]
    if other_subjects and len(triples) > len(primary_triples):
        for other_subject in other_subjects:
            other_triples = subject_triples[other_subject]
            if other_triples:
                other_tmpl = templates.get(other_triples[0].predicate, "has {predicate} {object}")
                other_clause = other_tmpl.format(object=other_triples[0].object)
                sentence += f" which {other_clause}"
                for t in other_triples[1:]:
                    tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate, "has {predicate} {object}")
                    clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
                    sentence += f", {clause}"

    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    sentence = sentence.strip()
    if not sentence.endswith("."):
        sentence += "."
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] if sentence else ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
